title: Deletion must include derived copies
description: Deleting a primary row is incomplete if caches, indexes, analytics, exports, embeddings, or denormalized records keep personal data.
body: Use when modifying deletion, retention, search, analytics, model indexing, or account lifecycle. Do not promise deletion for unmanaged systems without evidence. Actions: identify derived copies, add propagation or disassociation, and record asynchronous or manual gaps. Validation signals include before/after checks of derived stores. Risk boundary: inability to delete regulated or user-requested data requires human escalation. Use when Privacy Risk Controls work touches personal-data classes, sinks, retention, consent, logs, prompts, and exports. Do not use this guidance to override a more specific pack, user instruction, or repository contract. Actions: use the fact to constrain the change and test plan; inspect the current files first, keep the change scoped to the owning boundary, and record what evidence changed the decision. Validation signals include data inventory, redaction tests, retention checks, export/delete tests, and approval evidence. Risk boundary: escalate or stop when the task involves PII disclosure, consent bypass, retention drift, and unapproved processor expansion, missing owner evidence, or live external state.
